method_atom: A family of embedding/coarse-graining approximations for a large system of many strongly interacting units: partition the system into small finite sub-blocks, solve each block exactly by direct diagonalization of the finite subsystem's governing operator, then reconstruct the full system's response function either perturbatively (coupling the blocks) or by rendering a variational functional of the interaction self-correction stationary with respect to a small set of block parameters. Includes the numerical machinery for the exact finite-block solve and for evaluating the required integrals over conjugate (frequency and wavevector) variables.
object_atom: Strongly correlated quantum many-body systems — lattice models of interacting particles whose single-particle Green's functions and self-energies are sought — treated via cluster methods (Cluster Perturbation Theory, the Variational Cluster Approximation, and Cellular Dynamical Mean Field Theory built on Potthoff's self-energy functional formalism).
brief_reason: The solve-a-small-block-exactly-and-reconstruct-the-whole-via-a-stationary-functional embedding machinery is the transferable method, while the strongly correlated quantum lattice particles and their Green's-function/self-energy description are the physics-specific object.
